text: Gross income at or below 130% of poverty line; net income at or below 100% of poverty line. Earned-income deduction 20%, standard deduction, dependent-care deduction, excess medical for elderly/disabled, excess shelter deduction. Resource limit $2,750 (or $4,250 if elderly/disabled), excluding home + vehicle.

A SNAP household = individuals living together AND customarily purchasing food and preparing meals together. Spouses + minor children always count together. Elderly/disabled excluded from joint-household requirement if their income ≤ 165% poverty.

Able-Bodied Adults Without Dependents (18-49, no children, no disability) must work / participate in workfare / participate in employment-training program ≥ 80 hours/month, or limited to 3 months of SNAP in any 36-month period. State waivers available for high-unemployment areas.

Most categorically-needy adults under ACA Medicaid expansion: eligibility based on MAGI ≤ 138% poverty. Children: ≤ 138%-300% depending on state CHIP plan. Pregnant women: ≤ 138% (varies by state). MAGI excludes SSI/SSDI/child support.

5-year bar on most non-citizens for full-scope Medicaid; emergency Medicaid + treatment for serious medical conditions available regardless of status. Refugees, asylees, victims of trafficking are not subject to 5-year bar. State plans may extend coverage to lawfully residing children.

Resource limit: $2,000 (individual), $3,000 (couple). Excluded: primary home, one vehicle of any value, household goods/personal effects, life-insurance face value up to $1,500, burial fund up to $1,500. Above limit → ineligible until spend-down.

Monetary eligibility: claimant must have earned at least the state minimum wages in the base period (typically 4 of last 5 completed quarters). Base period earnings determine weekly benefit amount + max duration.

Voluntary quit without good cause = disqualified. Discharge for misconduct = disqualified. Reduction-in-force / layoff = eligible. Voluntary quit WITH good cause (medical, harassment, follow-spouse for military, domestic violence) = eligible. State varies.

Continued claim weeks require: able to work + available to work + actively seeking work (state-specified job-search activities per week). Failure to certify or report job offers = denied for that week.
Lifetime 60-month time limit on federal TANF assistance. States may extend with state-only funds; some states have shorter limits.

Applicant has 30 days to provide verification documentation. If verification not provided, application is denied with reapplication rights. Expedited (7-day) issuance for households with very low income + resources.

Adverse determinations come with mandatory appeal rights: notice of denial must include reason, applicable rule, deadline to appeal (typically 30-90 days depending on program), and contact for state hearing. Continued benefits during appeal in some programs (e.g. SNAP if appeal filed within 10 days of notice).
